cross_scene_arcs.title: The Struggle for Dignity Amid Economic and Social Marginalization
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Da Mayor's daily efforts to maintain dignity—through menial labor, adherence to routine, and asserting identity—are repeatedly undermined by economic precarity and shifting neighborhood dynamics, revealing his fragile social standing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Da Mayor relies on small acts of work to affirm his place in the community.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He asserts moral authority when challenged by youth, yet remains socially marginalized despite moments of respect.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His title and role are still questioned, reflecting broader community ambivalence toward aging, marginalized figures.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Recognition: The Hero's Reluctant Journey
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Da Mayor evolves from a socially rejected elder to a quietly heroic figure whose actions earn begrudging respect, particularly from Mother Sister, culminating in symbolic inclusion and care.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: He is publicly ridiculed and emotionally isolated by Mother Sister.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He is cared for in her home after surviving the riot, indicating a transformation in their relationship and his community status.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Mother Sister's acknowledgment is tempered with emotional restraint, leaving full reconciliation uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Authority in the Face of Collective Rage
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Da Mayor consistently positions himself as a voice of reason and moral clarity, attempting to de-escalate racial and communal tensions, but his influence is ultimately overwhelmed by systemic injustice and collective trauma.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: He offers wisdom to Mookie about doing the right thing, planting a seed of moral reflection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He actively intervenes to protect others during chaos, embodying the very principles he preached, even when ignored.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His appeals for peace fail to prevent violence, underscoring the limits of individual morality in the face of institutional oppression.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cycle of Survival and Self-Destruction
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Da Mayor's daily routine revolves around surviving the heat and earning money to buy alcohol, reflecting a cyclical existence where basic needs and addiction perpetuate his marginalization.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: He awakens physically strained by the oppressive heat, beginning another day of struggle.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He is seen asleep on his stoop, worn down and disengaged, trapped in a pattern of drinking and exhaustion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The cycle remains unbroken, with no clear path toward stability or recovery.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Workplace Loyalty
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mookie's relationship with Sal's Pizzeria deteriorates from professional resistance to full moral disengagement, as repeated confrontations with Pino and Sal expose racial and power-based tensions that erode his loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mookie asserts job boundaries by refusing to sweep, maintaining a functional but tense work role.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Mookie becomes a passive observer during Radio Raheem's death and later participates in the pizzeria's destruction, symbolizing his complete break from the workplace.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The systemic inequities and racial dynamics that fueled Mookie's alienation remain unaddressed, leaving the broader conflict between labor, race, and ownership unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Awakening and the Burden of Choice
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Guided by Da Mayor's moral advice and Radio Raheem's philosophy of Love and Hate, Mookie evolves from passive observer to active agent in the community uprising, culminating in a morally ambiguous resolution of his economic and ethical goals.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mookie receives the directive to 'always do the right thing,' beginning his internal moral reckoning.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Mookie retrieves his wages from Sal amid the ruins, symbolically balancing survival and justice but leaving the moral cost of his actions unresolved.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The ambiguity of what 'the right thing' means in a broken system remains unanswered, leaving Mookie's moral clarity incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Brotherhood and Familial Tensions
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mookie's relationship with his sister Jade shifts from playful intrusion to conflict over responsibility and control, reflecting deeper tensions around family, gender, and economic survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mookie affectionately invades Jade's space, establishing a dynamic of closeness with boundary issues.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Their relationship fractures over Mookie's jealousy and avoidance of responsibility, though Jade's decision to join him suggests a fragile reconnection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The power imbalance and emotional dependency in their sibling relationship remain unaddressed, with no clear resolution on mutual respect or independence.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fatherhood and Emotional Avoidance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mookie's interactions with Tina and his son Hector reveal a pattern of emotional withdrawal and prioritization of economic survival over familial presence, culminating in a cycle of absence and partial return.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mookie is confronted by Tina for neglecting his role as a father, exposing his emotional detachment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite brief intimacy and receiving wages, Mookie leaves Tina and Hector again, reinforcing his inability to sustain consistent responsibility.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Mookie's internal conflict between survival and fatherhood remains unresolved, with no indication of lasting change in his commitment to family.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Observer to Revolutionary
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Mookie transitions from an employee straddling two worlds to a symbolic participant in community resistance, ultimately reclaiming agency through destruction and economic reparation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Mookie is a detached observer during Sal and Radio Raheem's confrontation, embodying his liminal status.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He emerges as a figure of change, retrieving wages from the ruins and walking away as the neighborhood begins to heal.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term impact of Mookie's actions on the community and his own identity remains ambiguous, with his status as hero, survivor, or complicit figure left open to interpretation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Crumbling of Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sal begins as the unquestioned authority in his pizzeria, enforcing rules and mediating family and employee conflicts. Over time, his control is challenged by internal family tensions and external community resistance, culminating in the total collapse of his business and symbolic erasure of his legacy. His status shifts from dominant patriarch to powerless outsider.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal asserts dominance over Pino and maintains workplace order through physical and emotional authority.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sal's pizzeria is destroyed, his authority rejected by the community, and he is reduced to a passive observer of his own ruin.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sal never fully comprehends why his authority was dismantled, leaving his sense of identity and place in the community unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fatherhood Under Siege
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sal's relationship with his sons, particularly Pino and Vito, is defined by control, conflict, and unmet expectations. He attempts to assert paternal authority and unity, but is undermined by Pino's racism and Vito's passivity. His vision of a family legacy collapses under generational and ideological divides.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal disciplines Pino to enforce hierarchy, believing he can maintain family cohesion through dominance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: His sons react with horror to his 'Sal's and Sons' vision, revealing emotional alienation and the failure of his paternal ideal.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The rift between Sal and his sons—especially Pino's racism and Vito's silence—remains unhealed, leaving his dream of familial succession in ruins.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Wall of Fame and the Battle for Representation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The Wall of Fame becomes the focal point of a cultural and racial conflict between Sal and the Black community. What Sal sees as a personal tribute is perceived as exclusionary, sparking a boycott and escalating into symbolic and physical destruction. The conflict reflects deeper tensions over ownership, representation, and belonging.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal defends the Wall of Fame as his right as a business owner, dismissing Buggin' Out's demand for inclusion.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The Wall is consumed by fire, erasing Sal's cultural statement and marking the community's rejection of his narrative.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The question of who has the right to define cultural space in the neighborhood remains unanswered, with no reconciliation between Sal's pride and the community's demand for equity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mookie and the Fractured Loyalty
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The relationship between Sal and Mookie evolves from employer-employee to one of growing distrust and emotional distance. Personal tensions over Jade, financial strain, and unspoken accusations erode their bond, culminating in a transactional and adversarial final encounter.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal enforces workplace rules by denying Mookie early pay, asserting professional boundaries.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Mookie demands wages from Sal amid the ruins, receiving money under duress, symbolizing the end of loyalty and mutual respect.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral ambiguity of Mookie's role in the riot and Sal's complicity in systemic tensions remain unaddressed, leaving their relationship defined by loss and unresolved judgment.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Control to Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sal's central goal—to maintain his pizzeria as a family-run institution and a point of pride in the community—unfolds against mounting personal, racial, and social pressures. Despite repeated efforts to assert control, his goal is progressively undermined until it is utterly destroyed, both physically and symbolically.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sal seeks to preserve order, respect, and legacy within his business and family.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: His pizzeria is reduced to ashes, his legacy obliterated, and he withdraws emotionally and physically from the community.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Sal will attempt to rebuild, seek understanding, or remain estranged is left open, with his future goals undefined in the aftermath of total loss.
